zh.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Ros於大約1256年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾英格曼東普時，她的父親Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley28歲，母親Isabel d'Aubigny24歲。她於大約1285年與Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾英格曼東普
zh-hans.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Ros于大约1256年出生在英国英格兰越可爾英格曼東普时，她的父亲Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley28岁，母亲Isabel d'Aubigny24岁。她于大约1285年与Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Ros was born about 1256, in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, was 28 and her mother, Isabel d'Aubigny, was 24. She married Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge about 1285, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Ros est née environ 1256, à Ingmanthorpe, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Isabel d'Aubigny, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge environ 1285, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Ros um 1256 in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley 28 und ihre Mutter Isabel d'Aubigny 24. Sie hat um 1285 in Yorkshire, England Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Ros è nata il circa 1256 nel Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Isabel d'Aubigny, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge circa 1285, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Rosが約1256年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアイングマンソルペで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsleyは28歳，母Isabel d'Aubignyは24歳であった。約1285年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでWalter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconbergeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Ros(이)가 대략 1256년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 잉먼소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel d'Aubigny(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1285년 영국 요르크실르에서 Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England: Ingmanthorpe, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1256, em Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Isabel d'Aubigny, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge aproximadamente 1285, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Ros родилась около 1256 г. в местности ингмантаурп, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel d'Aubigny, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge около 1285 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Ros nació aproximadamente 1256, en Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Isabel d'Aubigny, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Walter de Fauconberge 2nd Lord Fauconberge aproximadamente 1285, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
